scope: Scope of Car Models Analyzed: 140 trims were analyzed across seven years & 17 car models.
count_note: Notes: Five vehicles are on the Watchlist (not yet available in the US): Toyota Highlander EV, Subaru 3-Row EV, BMW iX7, Genesis GV90, and Tesla Model Y Long (Asia). The Tesla Model Y (3-Row) has a child-sized third row. Rivian R1S Gen1 (2022â€“2024) and Gen2 (2025+) models are both included.

details.name: BMW iX7, 2027, xDrive50 (expected)
details.notes: MY2027. BMW's first full-size 3-row EV. Built at Spartanburg, SC. Expected late 2027. Specs not finalized.

details.name: BMW iX7, 2027, M70 (expected)
details.notes: MY2027. High-performance M variant. ~800+ HP expected. ~120 kWh battery, 400 kW DC fast charge capability. Expected late 2027.

details.name: Genesis GV90, 2027, Base (expected)
details.notes: Large 3-row electric SUV. Expected 2027-2028. ~$100k price point. Competitor to Lucid Gravity. Limited details announced.

glossary.meaning: Vehicle model year
glossary.notes: Example: 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026. This is the manufacturer's model year, not the calendar year of sale.

glossary.meaning: Specific trim / variant / seating configuration
glossary.notes: Identifies the exact version being compared, including battery, drivetrain, or seating differences where relevant.

glossary.meaning: Total seating capacity
glossary.notes: Total number of passengers the vehicle is configured to carry, such as 6 or 7.

glossary.meaning: Drive layout and motor configuration
glossary.notes: RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive) sends power to the rear wheels only â€” typically the most efficient layout with the longest range. AWD (All-Wheel Drive) uses two or more motors to power both axles, offering better traction, faster acceleration, and improved regen braking on both axles, at the cost of slightly reduced range. Tri-Motor AWD adds a third motor for even more power and torque distribution.

glossary.meaning: Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price
glossary.notes: Base sticker price before destination, taxes, dealer fees, incentives, or markups.

glossary.meaning: Factory destination / delivery charge
glossary.notes: Standard manufacturer freight/delivery fee added to MSRP on new vehicles.

glossary.meaning: Estimated used-market listing price range
glossary.notes: Estimated used-market listing price range based on publicly available pricing data.

glossary.meaning: Plain-English summary of driver assistance / autonomy capability
glossary.notes: General description of the vehicle's most advanced available hands-free / supervised driving system.

glossary.meaning: SAE J3016 driving automation level (0â€“5)
glossary.notes: Level 0 = no automation. Level 1 = one function automated (cruise OR lane keep). Level 2 = steering + speed automated but driver must supervise at all times. Level 3 = car drives in specific conditions, driver can look away. Level 4 = car drives itself in defined areas, no human needed. Level 5 = full automation everywhere. All vehicles in our dataset are currently Level 2.

glossary.meaning: Capability classification within SAE Level 2
glossary.notes: Basic L2: adaptive cruise + lane keep, hands-on required. Advanced L2: adds automated lane changes or multi-sensor suites, still hands-on. L2+ Hands-Free: hands-off on mapped highways, eyes-on required. L2+ Point-to-Point: city + highway navigation with automated turns and intersections, driver supervises.

glossary.meaning: EPA-rated or estimated driving range in miles
glossary.notes: Uses EPA range where available; otherwise estimated / reported range if EPA data is not yet available. Real-world range is typically 15â€“25% lower than the EPA number, especially in cold weather, at highway speeds, or with heavy cargo and climate control use.

glossary.meaning: Vehicle horsepower
glossary.notes: Manufacturer-reported or widely cited total system horsepower.

glossary.meaning: Battery pack size in kilowatt-hours
glossary.notes: Usually gross or commonly cited pack capacity; may be approximate depending on what manufacturers disclose.

glossary.meaning: Maximum onboard AC charging power
glossary.notes: Shows the car's AC charging hardware capability, which affects home / Level 2 charging speed.

glossary.meaning: Estimated Level 2 charging time from 10% to 80% in number of hours
glossary.notes: Derived estimate for the more practical 10%â€“80% AC charging window.

glossary.meaning: Native charging connector standard
glossary.notes: Example: NACS or CCS. Reflects the vehicle's native port, not just adapter compatibility.

glossary.meaning: Smartphone integration and software ecosystem
glossary.notes: Notes whether the vehicle supports Apple CarPlay / Android Auto, and if not, what native infotainment ecosystem it uses instead.

glossary.meaning: Primary infotainment touchscreen
glossary.notes: The main center-mounted touchscreen used for navigation, media, vehicle settings, and climate control.

glossary.meaning: Driver display behind the steering wheel
glossary.notes: Digital instrument cluster showing speed, range, and driving information. Some vehicles (e.g., Tesla) omit a dedicated cluster and display this info on the center screen.

glossary.meaning: Heads-up display availability
glossary.notes: Projects key driving information (speed, navigation) onto the windshield. Values: Yes (standard), Optional (available on certain trims/packages), or No.

glossary.meaning: Additional screens beyond center display and gauge cluster
glossary.notes: Includes rear-seat entertainment screens, HVAC control displays, passenger displays, or other secondary screens.

glossary.meaning: Audio / speaker system
glossary.notes: Describes the vehicle's sound system branding or speaker setup, where known.

glossary.meaning: Whether the vehicle supports distinct saved driver settings
glossary.notes: Indicates whether different drivers can save personalized settings such as seat, mirrors, steering, climate, and software preferences.

glossary.meaning: The cargo capacity of the front trunk (frunk), measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: A storage compartment under the front hood, made possible by the absence of a combustion engine. Size varies widely â€” from minimal (1â€“2 cu ft) to large enough for a carry-on suitcase (11+ cu ft).

glossary.meaning: The trunk/cargo volume behind the third row of seats when all seats are upright, measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: This is the everyday usable trunk space with all rows occupied. Typically the smallest cargo measurement since it only counts the area behind the rearmost seats.

glossary.meaning: The cargo volume behind the second row of seats when the third row is folded down or removed, measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: A substantially larger cargo area created by folding the 3rd row flat. Useful for bulky items like strollers, luggage, or furniture. Vehicles without a 3rd row (5-seat configurations) may not have this measurement.

glossary.meaning: The maximum cargo volume with all rear seats folded, measured from behind the front seats to the tailgate, in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: The largest possible cargo configuration. Useful for moving furniture, large appliances, or maximizing hauling capacity. This completes the cargo measurement trio: behind 3rd row (trunk), behind 2nd row (3rd folded), behind 1st row (everything folded).

glossary.meaning: Whether the rear seat rows fold completely flat to create a level cargo floor.
glossary.notes: Values: Yes (seats fold fully flat), No (seats do not fold flat, leaving a stepped surface), or Partial (some rows fold flat but not all, e.g. 2nd-row captain's chairs may not fold). A flat cargo floor makes it easier to slide in large or heavy items.

glossary.meaning: The width of the cargo floor between the interior wheel wells, measured in inches.
glossary.notes: This is the narrowest point of the cargo area and determines the widest items you can lay flat. Important for fitting wide objects like sheets of plywood, large boxes, or a mattress.

glossary.meaning: Source links used for that row
glossary.notes: URL list or source references used to build the row's data. Useful for validation and traceability.

glossary.meaning: Extra context / caveats
glossary.notes: Used for things like 'not yet released,' 'market data thin,' 'seat package optional,' or other row-specific caveats.

glossary.meaning: Maximum trailer weight the vehicle can tow (lbs)
glossary.notes: Based on manufacturer-rated braked towing capacity. Varies by drivetrain and equipment.

glossary.meaning: Maximum DC fast charging power the vehicle supports (kilowatts)
glossary.notes: Peak rate achievable at compatible stations (e.g., Supercharger, EA). Actual speed depends on battery state-of-charge and temperature.

glossary.meaning: Time to charge from 10% to 80% on a DC fast charger (minutes)
glossary.notes: Measured at or near the vehicle's peak charging rate. Real-world times may vary.

glossary.meaning: Weight of the vehicle with standard equipment and full fluids, no passengers or cargo (lbs)
glossary.notes: Varies slightly by seating configuration and options.

glossary.meaning: Overall bumper-to-bumper length of the vehicle (inches)
Overall width of the vehicle excluding side mirrors (inches)
Overall height of the vehicle at standard ride height (inches)
glossary.notes: Vehicles with air suspension may have adjustable height.

glossary.meaning: Distance from the lowest point of the vehicle to the ground (inches)
glossary.notes: Measured at standard ride height. Vehicles with air suspension may have adjustable clearance.

glossary.meaning: Available legroom in the third row of seats (inches)
glossary.notes: Key metric for 3-row vehicles. Measured with 2nd row in standard position.

glossary.meaning: Available headroom in the third row of seats (inches)
Maximum rotational force produced by the motor(s) (lb-ft)
glossary.notes: Electric motors deliver peak torque instantly. Relevant for towing and acceleration.

glossary.meaning: Time to accelerate from 0 to 60 miles per hour (seconds)
glossary.notes: Manufacturer-claimed or independently tested. Lower is faster.

assumptions.notes: PA base sales tax (Delaware County is 6%)
Typical PA doc fee estimate; varies by dealer
PA annual fee estimate for EVs; check current schedule
